key_insight: The paper proves optimal-order $H^1$ error bounds for first- and second-order $A$-stable backward difference discretizations of Willmore flow on closed surfaces.
engineering_relevance: It provides a rigorous stability and convergence foundation for reliable high-order finite element simulation of evolving geometric surfaces in computational mechanics.
method: Evolve the coupled Willmore flow system with surface finite elements of degree at least 2 in space.
Use backward difference methods of order 1 or 2 in time with $A$-stability.
Prove stability via energy estimates, Dahlquist $G$-stability, and Nevanlinna–Odeh multiplier techniques.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments that illustrate and complement the theoretical error analysis.
watch_item: The analysis assumes closed two-dimensional surfaces and sufficiently high polynomial degree, so implementation on open or lower-order meshes may need additional care.
paper_title: Error estimates for $A$-stable backward difference full discretizations of Willmore flow of closed surfaces
